persons.name: Marta Cabrera
persons.notes: Cannot lie without vomiting

deaths.cause_detail: Self-inflicted throat wound with a medical lancet after falsely believing he had fatally overdosed Marta with morphine; Ransom had swapped the medication vials to frame Marta, but Marta administered the correct drug
deaths.motive_detail: Harlan sacrificed himself to protect Marta from prosecution
deaths.notes: The twist: Harlan's death was unnecessary — Marta actually gave the correct medication, having recognised the viscosity difference. Ransom orchestrated the situation to make Marta appear guilty and disinherit her.

deaths.cause_detail: Administered a fatal morphine overdose by Ransom Drysdale after she attempted to blackmail him about tampering with the Thrombey evidence
deaths.motive_detail: Fran had discovered Ransom swapped the medication vials and threatened blackmail

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
creator: Rian Johnson

persons.name: Miles Bron
persons.notes: Creator of Klear, an unstable hydrogen fuel

persons.name: Andi Brand
persons.notes: Co-founder of Miles's company; murdered before the events of the film

persons.name: Helen Brand
persons.notes: Andi's identical twin sister, impersonating Andi at the island party

persons.name: Duke Cody
persons.notes: Severely allergic to pineapple

deaths.cause_detail: Miles Bron drugged Andi's drink, rendered her unconscious, placed her in her car in a closed garage with the engine running, staging it as a suicide
deaths.motive_detail: Andi had found the original napkin proving she co-created Miles's company and was about to expose him
deaths.notes: Death occurred approximately one week before the island party; initially ruled a suicide

deaths.cause_detail: Miles secretly introduced pineapple juice into Duke's drink, triggering a fatal allergic reaction after Duke discovered Miles had killed Andi and attempted blackmail
deaths.motive_detail: Duke threatened to expose Miles as Andi's killer

deaths.cause_detail: Miles shot Helen during the confrontation on the island; the bullet was stopped by Andi's journal in Helen's jacket pocket
deaths.motive_detail: Helen was unmasking Miles as the killer
deaths.notes: Helen survived — the bullet was stopped by a journal

title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery
creator: Rian Johnson

persons.name: Martha Delacroix
persons.notes: Orchestrated the conspiracy; ultimately sacrifices herself

persons.name: Samson Holt
persons.notes: Martha Delacroix's lover

deaths.cause_detail: Nat Sharp poisoned Wicks's flask to render him unconscious, then replaced a decoy finial attached to his red vestment with a real knife blade, killing him during Good Friday services
deaths.motive_detail: To secure the 'Eve's Apple' diamond hidden on Prentice Wicks's corpse

deaths.cause_detail: Killed by Nat Sharp to prevent him from keeping the Eve's Apple diamond he had retrieved
deaths.motive_detail: Nat killed Samson to take sole possession of the Eve's Apple diamond

deaths.cause_detail: Martha Delacroix swapped their coffee cups, poisoning Nat with pentobarbital
deaths.motive_detail: Martha ended Nat's life as part of her final act of confession and penance

deaths.cause_detail: Martha deliberately ingested pentobarbital after confessing her role in the conspiracy, seeking forgiveness

persons.name: Evelyn Howard
persons.notes: Appeared to be Emily's loyal friend; was Alfred's secret accomplice and lover

persons.name: Arthur Hastings
persons.notes: Narrator of the novel

deaths.cause_detail: Strychnine introduced into Emily's bromide sleeping powder by Alfred Inglethorp and Evelyn Howard; the drug reacted with a tonic Emily was also taking to produce delayed fatal convulsions
deaths.motive_detail: Emily had made a will leaving her estate to Alfred; he and Evelyn Howard conspired to hasten her death
deaths.notes: Evelyn Howard was Alfred's secret accomplice; the delayed reaction of the poison created a mystery about timing

persons.name: Paul Renauld
persons.notes: Real name Georges Conneau; had a criminal past in Canada

persons.name: Madame Daubreuil
persons.notes: Also known as Jeanne Beroldy; was blackmailing Renauld about his past

persons.name: Jack Renauld
persons.notes: In love with Marthe Daubreuil; confessed to killing father to protect Marthe

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed and placed in a makeshift grave on the golf course near his home; initially complicated by the appearance of a second body and multiple false confessions
deaths.motive_detail: Renauld was attempting to escape Daubreuil's blackmail; she killed him when the plan to fake his own death failed
deaths.notes: The case involves a second unidentified female body and a false confession by Jack Renauld

persons.name: Dr. James Sheppard
persons.notes: The narrator of the novel; revealed to be the killer in the famous twist

persons.name: Mrs. Ferrars
persons.notes: Died before the novel begins; had been blackmailed by Dr. Sheppard

persons.name: Ralph Paton
persons.notes: Ackroyd's stepson; primary suspect due to disappearance and secret marriage

deaths.cause_detail: Took a fatal overdose of veronal the night before the main action; had been poisoned — or driven to suicide — having confessed to murdering her own husband and been blackmailed by Dr. Sheppard
deaths.motive_detail: Could no longer bear the blackmail; had confessed to Ackroyd in a letter
deaths.notes: Backstory death; she had also killed her first husband (Mr. Ferrars) with arsenic

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed in the neck with an ornamental Tunisian dagger while alone in his locked study after reading Mrs. Ferrars's confession letter
deaths.motive_detail: Ackroyd had just read Ferrars's letter naming Sheppard as her blackmailer; Sheppard killed him before he could act on the information
deaths.notes: The narrator of the novel is the killer — one of crime fiction's most famous twists

title: The Big Four
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: More spy thriller than classic detective novel; episodic structure adapted from short stories

persons.name: Li Chang Yen
persons.notes: Leader of the Big Four international crime syndicate

persons.name: Madame Olivier
persons.notes: Number Three of the Big Four

persons.name: Abe Ryland
persons.notes: Number Two of the Big Four

deaths.cause_detail: Killed in the explosion that destroys the Big Four's underground headquarters
deaths.motive_detail: Poirot destroyed the headquarters to eliminate the criminal organisation
deaths.notes: Multiple Big Four operatives die across the episodic narrative

deaths.cause_detail: Killed in the destruction of the Big Four's headquarters
deaths.motive_detail: Elimination of the Big Four syndicate

title: The Mystery of the Blue Train
creator: Agatha Christie

persons.name: Ruth Kettering
persons.notes: Daughter of millionaire Rufus Van Aldin; travelling to Riviera

persons.name: Derek Kettering
persons.notes: Suspect but ultimately not the killer

persons.name: Ada Mason
persons.notes: Real name Kitty Kidd; working with jewel thief the Comte de la Roche's associate

persons.name: Mirelle
persons.notes: Derek Kettering's mistress

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled in her private compartment on the Blue Train overnight; her jewels (the Heart of Fire rubies) stolen; her face battered to delay identification
deaths.motive_detail: Ada Mason and her accomplice killed Ruth to steal the valuable Heart of Fire ruby necklace

persons.name: Nick Buckley
persons.notes: Staged multiple apparent attempts on her own life to create an alibi for murder

persons.name: Maggie Buckley
persons.notes: Nick's cousin; killed by mistake while wearing Nick's shawl

persons.name: Freddie Rice
persons.notes: Nick's drug-addicted friend; unwitting pawn

deaths.cause_detail: Shot at a fireworks party while wearing Nick Buckley's shawl; the killer intended to shoot Nick
deaths.motive_detail: Nick wanted to inherit from her cousin Michael Seton; she needed to appear as a near-murder victim while actually eliminating the real beneficiary
deaths.notes: Twist: the apparent victim (Nick) was actually the killer staging attempts on her own life

persons.name: Lord Edgware
persons.notes: Refused to grant Jane Wilkinson a divorce

persons.name: Jane Wilkinson
persons.notes: The real killer despite a seemingly perfect alibi

persons.name: Carlotta Adams
persons.notes: Hired to impersonate Jane Wilkinson; killed to silence her

persons.name: Ronald Marsh
persons.notes: Lord Edgware's nephew; stood to inherit the title

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed in the neck in his study; Jane Wilkinson entered disguised as herself (having arranged for Carlotta Adams to impersonate her at a dinner party across town to establish an alibi)
deaths.motive_detail: Jane wanted to be free to marry an American duke; Lord Edgware refused to divorce her

deaths.cause_detail: Found dead from a veronal overdose, staged as accidental; she had been paid to impersonate Jane Wilkinson and had to be silenced
deaths.motive_detail: Carlotta could identify Jane as the one who hired her to create the false alibi

persons.name: Samuel Ratchett
persons.notes: Real name Cassetti; kidnapped and caused the death of Daisy Armstrong

persons.name: Hector MacQueen
persons.notes: Son of D.A. MacQueen who was ruined by the Armstrong case

persons.name: Princess Dragomiroff
persons.notes: Godmother of Sonia Armstrong

persons.name: Mary Debenham
persons.notes: Was governess in the Armstrong household

persons.name: Daisy Armstrong
persons.notes: Kidnapped and murdered by Cassetti/Ratchett; her death set the entire revenge plot in motion

deaths.cause_detail: Three-year-old kidnapped and murdered by Cassetti for ransom; death occurred years before the main action
deaths.motive_detail: Ransom kidnapping that ended in the child's murder
deaths.notes: Backstory death; the motive for the entire revenge plot on the Orient Express

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed twelve times in his locked sleeping compartment overnight while the snowbound train was halted; each of the twelve passengers connected to the Armstrong case dealt one blow
deaths.motive_detail: Collective revenge by twelve people connected to the Armstrong kidnapping case for Cassetti's escape from justice
deaths.notes: Twist: all twelve passengers are guilty; some wounds were post-mortem; Poirot presents two solutions and allows the passengers to go free

persons.name: Sir Charles Cartwright
persons.notes: Famous actor; the killer

persons.name: Reverend Babbington
persons.notes: Killed because he recognised Charles from years earlier when Charles used a different identity

persons.name: Dr. Bartholomew Strange
persons.notes: Killed because he had begun to suspect Cartwright

persons.name: Hermione Lytton Gore
persons.notes: Cartwright's romantic obsession; her mother Lady Mary was intended to be a victim

deaths.cause_detail: Nicotine introduced into his cocktail glass at a dinner party hosted by Sir Charles Cartwright; appeared to be sudden natural death
deaths.motive_detail: Babbington had known Cartwright years earlier under a different identity and was on the verge of recognising him

deaths.cause_detail: Nicotine in his cocktail at his own house party; staged to mirror Babbington's death and appear as a copycat or natural cluster
deaths.motive_detail: Strange had become suspicious of Cartwright

persons.name: Madame Giselle
persons.notes: Held financial secrets over many of the passengers

persons.name: Norman Gale
persons.notes: Used a blowpipe disguised among the wasp activity on the plane

deaths.cause_detail: Shot with a poisoned dart from a blowpipe while aboard an airliner over the English Channel; the dart was disguised as a wasp sting
deaths.motive_detail: Gale was indebted to Giselle and killed her to erase the debt and prevent exposure

persons.name: Franklin Clarke
persons.notes: Killed his brother Sir Carmichael Clarke; created the ABC serial killer pattern as cover

persons.name: Sir Carmichael Clarke
persons.notes: Franklin's brother; the only intended victim

persons.name: Alexander Cust
persons.notes: Epileptic travelling salesman; framed by Clarke to appear as the ABC killer

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned in her tobacco and newsagent shop in Andover; an ABC railway guide left open at Andover
deaths.motive_detail: Killed to establish the ABC serial pattern and distract from the true target (Sir Carmichael Clarke)

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled on Bexhill beach; ABC guide left open at Bexhill
deaths.motive_detail: Second killing to reinforce the ABC serial killer pattern

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned at his estate in Churston; the true intended target of the entire scheme
deaths.motive_detail: Franklin stood to inherit his brother's considerable estate
deaths.notes: Twist: the apparent serial ABC murders were cover for a single inheritance murder

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed at a cinema in Doncaster; wrong victim — Clarke intended to kill Donald Fraser who was investigating him
deaths.motive_detail: Continuing the ABC pattern; wrong victim was stabbed opportunistically

persons.name: Louise Leidner
persons.notes: Had been receiving threatening letters; her first husband was Frederick Bosner

persons.name: Dr. Eric Leidner
persons.notes: Is actually Frederick Bosner, Louise's believed-dead first husband

persons.name: Nurse Amy Leatheran
persons.notes: Narrator of the novel

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned in her room using a rubber sandbag; the killer entered through the window using a specially constructed device
deaths.motive_detail: Louise was about to expose Dr. Leidner as her first husband Frederick Bosner, who had been a German spy and was believed executed
deaths.notes: Twist: the threatening letters Louise received were sent by her own husband, who was her supposedly dead first husband in disguise

persons.name: Mr. Shaitana
persons.notes: Hosted a dinner party where the four suspected murderers played bridge; stabbed during the game

persons.name: Dr. Roberts
persons.notes: The killer; had previously killed patients

persons.name: Anne Meredith
persons.notes: Had killed a previous employer by poisoning

persons.name: Mrs. Lorrimer
persons.notes: Confessed falsely to protect another; died by her own hand

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed with a stiletto while seated in his armchair during a bridge game; the murder occurred in the same room as four other people, all playing cards
deaths.motive_detail: Shaitana had deduced Roberts was a murderer and was publicly exhibiting him as such

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned (administered by Dr. Roberts who attended her as her physician) after she sent a false confession to police to protect someone else
deaths.motive_detail: Roberts poisoned Lorrimer to silence her and prevent her false confession from exposing him

persons.name: Linnet Ridgeway Doyle
persons.notes: Married her best friend's fiancé Simon Doyle

persons.name: Simon Doyle
persons.notes: Shot Linnet while Jacqueline was establishing his alibi; also killed Louise the maid

persons.name: Jacqueline de Bellefort
persons.notes: Simon's true love; conspired with him to murder Linnet for her fortune

persons.name: Louise Bourget
persons.notes: Witnessed the murder and attempted blackmail

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the temple at close range while asleep in her cabin; Jacqueline shot Simon earlier that evening (a staged wound) to give him an alibi, after which Simon retrieved the gun and shot Linnet
deaths.motive_detail: Simon and Jacqueline planned to murder Linnet so Simon could inherit her fortune and reunite with Jacqueline
deaths.notes: Twist: Simon's leg wound was real but self-inflicted; his alibi (being with the doctor while Jacqueline appeared in the lounge) was carefully staged

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed through the throat while lying in her bunk; she had signalled she knew the identity of Linnet's killer and was attempting blackmail
deaths.motive_detail: Louise witnessed events that implicated Simon and attempted to extort money

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Jacqueline de Bellefort in their cabin after Poirot reveals the truth; a mercy killing and murder-suicide pact
deaths.motive_detail: Jacqueline killed Simon before turning the gun on herself; neither could face justice

deaths.cause_detail: Shot herself immediately after killing Simon Doyle

persons.name: Emily Arundell
persons.notes: Changed her will to leave everything to her companion Minnie Lawson shortly before dying

persons.name: Minnie Lawson
persons.notes: Poisoned Emily with phosphorus in her medicine; had befriended Emily to inherit her estate

deaths.cause_detail: Phosphorus introduced into Emily's liver pills by Minnie Lawson; caused jaundice and eventual death, appearing as natural liver failure
deaths.motive_detail: Lawson had manipulated Emily into changing her will and then poisoned her to inherit the estate
deaths.notes: Emily had written to Poirot before dying, suspecting one of her relatives; Poirot investigates after Emily is already dead

persons.name: Mrs. Boynton
persons.notes: Former prison wardress; psychologically abused her family for years

persons.name: Lady Westholme
persons.notes: Former American criminal (pickpocket Althea Boynton); killed to prevent exposure of her past

deaths.cause_detail: Injected with a fatal dose of digitoxin in her wrist while seated outside her tent in the ruins of Petra; her existing heart condition masked the murder
deaths.motive_detail: Mrs. Boynton recognised Lady Westholme from her criminal past in America and was about to expose her

persons.name: Simeon Lee
persons.notes: Had an illegitimate son he didn't know; gathered his family for Christmas

persons.name: Superintendent Sugden
persons.notes: Illegitimate son of Simeon Lee; used his police role to control the investigation

persons.name: Alfred Lee
persons.notes: The loyal son who genuinely cared for his father

deaths.cause_detail: Throat cut and body badly mutilated in his locked room on Christmas Eve; the locked-room puzzle is created by a trick with a key
deaths.motive_detail: Sugden is Simeon Lee's illegitimate son; Lee had seduced and abandoned Sugden's mother
deaths.notes: Twist: the investigating police officer is the killer and Lee's own illegitimate son
